calendar.matters:
SMALL CLAIM APPEALS
Off Calendar Apr-05-2019 Judgment Entered
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jessica Alexandra, Esq., attorney for and with Plaintiff/Respondent Ryan Conrad; Karen Uchiyama, Esq. attorney for and with Defendant/Appellant Visit Taveethamcharoen.

This proceeding is not reported.

AM: Court and counsel review the posture of the case, including the appealabilty of the Cross-complaint. After discussion and review of CCP Sec. 116.710 and the Notice of Entry of Judgment, the Court will allow appeal of the judgment on the Cross-complaint.

At the Court's request, the parties and three proposed witness are placed under oath.

Ms. Uchiyama provides opening statement on behalf of Appellant Taveethamcharoen followed by Ms. Alexandra on behalf of Respondent Conrad.

Testimony commences with direct of Mr.Visit Taveethamcharoen, followed by cross by Ms. Alexandra.

PM: Brief redirect by Ms. Uchiyama who rests, reserving Evidence Code Sec. 776 examination of the Plaintiff/Respondent Conrad.

Testimony of Mr. Ryan Conrad on direct by Ms. Alexandra, followed by cross by Ms. Uchiyama.

Testimony of Jason Quashnofski on direct by Ms. Alexandra, followed by cross by Ms. Uchiyama.

Testimony of Eva Silversteen on direct by Ms. Alexandra, followed by cross by Ms. Uchiyama.

Exhibits:

Appellant Exhibits

A: Lease
B: Cashier's Check - security deposit
C: Police Report
D: Receipt - broken window repair
E: Check - reimbursement for broken window
F: Water bill
G: Photos - termination walk-through inspection
H: Repair bill invoice
I: Photo of broken door
J: Check for cleaning
K: Calculation of security deposit interest
L: Ryan Letter to Taveethamcharoen re: termination of tenancy
M: Taveethamcharoen notes from termination walk-through inspection
N: Condition report - property manager inspection report at the commencement of the tenancy

Exhibits A-M are admitted into evidence.

Respondent Exhibits

1: Repair bill invoice - Appellant's Exhibit H with additional pages and interlineations
2: Photos - termination walk-through inspection [Plaintiff/Respondent photos]
3: E-mail - Conrad to Taveethamcharoen
4: E-mail - Conrad to Taveethamcharoen

Judge: Hon. Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Reporter: not reported. (D306)

SMALL CLAIMS APPEAL NOTICE OF TIME AND PLACE
Off Calendar Apr-04-2019 Set For Trial On Apr-04-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 306

SMALL CLAIM APPEALS
Off Calendar Apr-04-2019 Continued To Apr-04-19 At 09:15 A.M. In Dept 206
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Jessica Alexandra, Attorney at Law, representing plaintiff/respondent is present;
Ryan Conrad, for plaintiff/respondent is also present;
Karen Y. Uchiyama, Attorney at Law, representing defendant/appellant is present;
Visit Taveethamcharoen, for defendant/appellant is also present.

This case comes on calendar regularly for trial de novo. All parties and witnesses are sworn.

The parties would not stipulate to the Judge Pro Tem. The matter is transferred to the Master Calendar Department, Dept. 206, for reassignment.

Judge Pro Tem: Elliott Myles; Clerk: Felicia Green; Not reported; Dept. 525.

SMALL CLAIM APPEALS
Off Calendar Mar-20-2019 Continued To Apr-04-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 525
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Jessica Alexandra, attorney at law, representing Plaintiff/Respondent is present;
Ryan Conrad, for Plaintiff/Respondent is also present;
Visit Taveethamcharoen, for Defendant/Appellant is present.

This cause comes on regularly for trial de novo. All parties and witnesses are sworn.

Defendant/Appellant informs the Court that his attorney is not present. After numerous attempts, Defendant/Appellant is not able to reach his attorney. Defendant/Appellant requests a continuance of the matter. The Court GRANTS Defendants/Appellants request for continuance and informs Defendant/Appellant that no further contiuances will be allowed, absent truly exceptional circumstances. Attorney's for both parties are instructed to file their Substitution of Attorney forms before the next hearing date.

The matter is continued to April-04-2019, at 9:00 a.m., by agreement of the parties.

Judge Pro Tem: Diana Kruze; Clerk: Felicia Green; Not reported; Dept. 525.
